dosing_enrichment.titration_note: Begin AOD-9604 at 150 mcg/day for weeks 1-2 to assess tolerability, then increase to 300 mcg/day weeks 3+. Introduce MOTS-c at 5 mg 3x/week from week 1. Monitor fasting glucose weekly for first 4 weeks.
dosing_enrichment.cycling_recommendation: 12-week active cycles followed by 4-6 week washout for peptide components (BPC-157, MOTS-c). AOD-9604 may be used continuously. Metformin (if included) continuous use as directed.
dosing_enrichment.maintenance_dose: AOD-9604: 300 mcg subcutaneous daily (morning, fasted). MOTS-c: 5 mg subcutaneous 3x/week. BPC-157 (GI support): 250 mcg subcutaneous daily.
dosing_enrichment.timing_optimization: AOD-9604 and MOTS-c should be administered in a fasted state (60-90 min pre-meal) to maximize lipolytic and insulin-sensitizing effects. Avoid post-meal administration.

protocol_author_notes: Official curated protocol for general clinical use.

qualitative: Improved fasting glucose and insulin sensitivity

quantitative_ranges.week_8: Waist circumference decrease 2-4 cm; triglycerides reduction 10-20%; fasting insulin decrease 15-25%
quantitative_ranges.week_4: Fasting glucose decrease 5-10%; subjective energy and satiety improvement in 60-70% of patients
quantitative_ranges.week_12: Visceral fat area reduction 8-15% (MRI-measured); HbA1c improvement 0.5-0.8%; HOMA-IR decrease 25-35%

expected_outcomes.responder_rate_pct: 70-75% for metabolic markers improvement; 60-65% for body composition change

overview_summary: 10-week MOTS-c & AOD-9604 Metabolic Activation Protocol targeting metabolic health through peptide-mediated metabolic signalling, with structured phase progression, monitoring checkpoints, and conservative titration.

special_notes: Use conservative ramping if sensitivity profile is high.
Reassess tolerance and response before escalation.

legacy_compatibility.notes: Legacy fields may still be generated temporarily for backward compatibility while the generator transitions to blueprint + resolved output mode.

monitoringSchedule.label: End-of-Protocol Evaluation
monitoringSchedule.notes: Full outcome assessment and decision on continuation or cycling.

contraindications: Personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma (GLP-1 class)

side_effects.management: Rotate sites; use proper aseptic technique.
Reduce dose; monitor blood pressure; salt restriction.
Dose with food; split doses; titrate slowly.
Monitor fasting glucose weekly; consult if persistent.
Reduce dose; psychological support if needed.

escalation_criteria: Severe or worsening edema — STOP, cardiovascular evaluation
Fasting glucose > 7.0 mmol/L (126 mg/dL) on two occasions — Endocrinology referral
Hypertensive urgency (BP > 180/110) — STOP, immediate medical attention
riskManagement.storage_handling: Store peptides at 2–8°C (refrigerated). Reconstituted vials: stable for 28 days refrigerated. Do not freeze reconstituted product. Protect from light.
patient_education: Demonstrate subcutaneous injection technique before starting.
Provide written dosing schedule and injection site rotation map.
Instruct patient to report any unusual symptoms within 24h.
Provide emergency contact number for out-of-hours concerns.

clinical_disclaimer: For use by qualified healthcare professionals only. Not a diagnosis, prescription, or substitute for individualized medical judgment, informed consent, and local regulatory compliance.

evidence_limitations: Several peptide uses in this library remain off-label, investigational, exploratory, or based on translational evidence.
Protocol data should not be interpreted as individualized medical advice or a substitute for prescribing clinician judgment.
Regulatory status varies by country and must be checked locally before clinical use or commercial distribution.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.update_scope: missing clinical/regulatory/safety information added while preserving canonical antigravity_v2 data model
latest_clinical_notes: AOD-9604 and MOTS-c remain non-approved for obesity/metabolic disease; avoid presenting as substitutes for approved obesity medicines.
Monitor glucose, HbA1c, lipids, hepatic enzymes, renal function, and body composition.
Metabolic protocols should include nutrition, resistance training, and medication review for diabetes therapies.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.clinical_use_guardrail: Use under qualified medical supervision where legally permitted; obtain informed consent for off-label or investigational use.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.clinical_use_note: This protocol is informational and designed for clinician-supervised review. It is not a standalone prescription, diagnosis, or patient-specific treatment plan.

metadata.longDescription: MOTS-c (mitochondrial open reading frame of the 12S rRNA-c) is an endogenous mitochondria-derived peptide that translocates to the nucleus under metabolic stress, activating AMPK and the AICAR pathway to improve insulin sensitivity and glucose homeostasis (Lee C et al., Cell Metab 2015; Reynolds JC et al., Nat Aging 2021). AOD-9604, the C-terminal fragment of human growth hormone (hGH 176–191), promotes lipolysis via β-adrenergic receptor pathways without inducing IGF-1 or insulin resistance (Ng FM et al., Biochem Mol Biol Int 1997). Combined, this 10-week protocol targets adipose reduction and metabolic flexibility in individuals with metabolic syndrome, pre-diabetes, or dyslipidaemia. The two-phase structure begins with full dual-compound activation then transitions to MOTS-c maintenance for sustained mitochondrial benefit.
metadata.target_patient_profile: Adults 30-65 with metabolic syndrome, central obesity (waist >94 cm men, >80 cm women), insulin resistance (HOMA-IR >2.5), or pre-diabetes. Ideal for patients who have plateaued on diet/exercise interventions and need targeted metabolic support without GLP-1 agonist commitment.
metadata.short_description: A 12-week multi-peptide protocol targeting visceral fat, insulin resistance, and mitochondrial metabolic efficiency using AOD-9604, MOTS-c, and optional BPC-157.

references.citation: Lee C et al. The Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide MOTS-c Promotes Metabolic Homeostasis. Cell Metab 2015;21:443-454.
Reynolds JC et al. MOTS-c is an exercise-induced mitochondrial-encoded regulator of age-dependent physical decline. Nat Aging 2021;1:147-159.
Ng FM et al. Metabolic studies of a synthetic lipolytic domain (AOD9604) of human growth hormone. Biochem Mol Biol Int 1997;43:993-1000.

metadata.scientificName: MOTS-c & AOD-9604 AMPK-Driven Metabolic Activation Protocol
metadata.evidence_grade_note: GRADE B: AOD-9604 Phase II trials (Metabolic Pharmaceuticals 2004); MOTS-c preclinical and early Phase I data (Lee C et al., Cell Metab 2015). Robust mechanistic evidence; limited large RCT data for peptide components.
metadata.description: 10-week metabolic health protocol combining MOTS-c mitochondrial AMPK activation with AOD-9604 selective lipolysis to restore insulin sensitivity and reduce adiposity.
metadata.clinical_summary: This metabolic optimization protocol combines AOD-9604 (a lipolytic GH fragment), MOTS-c (a mitochondrial peptide that activates AMPK and improves insulin sensitivity via skeletal muscle glucose uptake), and optional BPC-157 for gut-metabolic axis support. AOD-9604 acts on beta-3 adrenergic receptors to stimulate lipolysis without IGF-1 effects. MOTS-c mimics exercise-induced mitochondrial signaling, improving glucose homeostasis and reducing visceral fat accumulation.

pubmed_references.title: MOTS-c: A Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide Regulating Muscle and Fat Metabolism
Oral administration of AOD9604 for 12 weeks: effects on body composition in obese adults
Gut microbiota and metabolic syndrome: the GLP-1 connection

metadata.source_quality_note: Clinical content consolidated from protocol library and public biomedical literature; final medical use requires qualified clinician review.
metadata.regulatory_note: AOD-9604: Not FDA-approved for obesity; listed as a food ingredient by FDA (GRAS status disputed). Available via compounding pharmacies as research peptide. MOTS-c: not approved; research use only. Use under physician supervision. Updated 2026: AOD-9604 and MOTS-c are not FDA/EMA-approved for obesity, insulin resistance, or metabolic syndrome treatment.

drug_interactions: Insulin and insulin secretagogues: additive glucose-lowering - adjust doses carefully
Metformin (combination use): additive AMPK activation - generally synergistic, monitor for GI side effects
Thyroid medications: AOD-9604 may affect thyroid axis indirectly - monitor TSH annually
monitoring_required: Fasting glucose and fasting insulin (HOMA-IR) at baseline, week 4, week 12
Waist circumference and body composition (DEXA if available) at baseline and week 12
Lipid panel at baseline and week 12
HbA1c at baseline and week 12 (pre-diabetic patients)
adverse_events_serious: No serious adverse events reported in AOD-9604 Phase II trials at standard doses
MOTS-c: no serious adverse events in human Phase I data to date
Theoretical hypoglycemia risk if combined with insulin or sulfonylureas - monitor
adverse_events_common: Injection site reactions: 5-15% - mild erythema/bruising, rotate sites
Mild hypoglycemia: possible if fasted state prolonged or combined with insulin sensitizers (5-8%)
Transient fatigue or GI discomfort in first 2 weeks (10%)
Appetite suppression - usually desired effect but monitor in low-BMI patients
